description: Eval set for django-inquisition:optimise-orm. Each eval points at a self-contained fixture under ../tests/fixtures/. Each fixture has a target.py (the file the skill should analyse), settings.py (DB engine + INSTALLED_APPS context), models.py (where applicable), and expected.json (the required findings the skill must emit). The richer per-fixture expectations live in expected.json — this evals.json is the skill-creator-compatible pointer surface.

evals.name: basic-n-plus-one
evals.expected_output: FETCH-030 (N+1 in template) + FETCH-001 (missing select_related) appear as 🔥 Critical findings

evals.name: bulk-write-loop
evals.expected_output: WRITE-001 (loop of .save()) appears as 🔥 Critical with bulk_create fix template

evals.name: missing-prefetch
evals.expected_output: FETCH-010, FETCH-011, FETCH-012 surface with prefetch_related/Prefetch fix templates

evals.name: column-overfetching-with-callers
evals.expected_output: FETCH-020/021/022 surface; caller-grep against callers.py informs confidence

evals.name: missing-index-postgres
evals.expected_output: IDX-001, IDX-002, IDX-010, IDX-040 surface with PG-aware suggestions (GIN, composite)

evals.name: audit-framework-bypass-easyaudit
evals.expected_output: PAT-070 banner detected; WRITE-006/007/009 escalated from medium to 🔥 Critical due to easyaudit in INSTALLED_APPS; signals_caveat blocks present

evals.name: suppression-marker
evals.expected_output: Lines with `# noqa: optimise-orm WRITE-006` are suppressed from findings; report frontmatter shows suppressed: N count

evals.name: mysql-engine-degradation
evals.expected_output: IDX-040 (GIN suggestion) demoted to info banner because engine is MySQL, not PG

evals.name: non-django-target-clean-exit
evals.expected_output: Skill exits cleanly with `No Django ORM usage detected. Nothing to analyse.`

evals.name: symbol-resolution-ambiguous
evals.prompt: Audit /django-inquisition:optimise-orm OrderListView — verify disambiguation prompt when symbol matches multiple files
evals.expected_output: Skill lists matching files and asks user to pick a dotted path before continuing

evals.name: pghistory-no-bypass-warning
evals.expected_output: WRITE-006 does NOT escalate because pghistory uses PG triggers (signals_safe=true); no signals_caveat block

evals.name: async-orm-django-4-1
evals.expected_output: PAT-050 surfaces — sync ORM call inside async def view; suggest aget/afilter (Django ≥ 4.1)

notes: The richer machine-readable expectations live in each fixture's expected.json (with severity_internal, tier_displayed, location_pattern, savings_basis, signals_caveat). Live runs should use tests/run.sh --live which invokes the skill against each fixture and diffs findings against expected.json. The evals here are the skill-creator-compatible pointer set.
